title: My Orders clustering — group orders into Movies and TV Shows sections
commit_message: feat: cluster My Orders screen by content type — add ORDER_TYPES, getOrdersByType, and render Movies/TVShows sections separately
changed_symbols: getOrdersByType (new helper — filters orders array by type field)
myOrderDetails (modified — type field added to every order entry)
MyOrder (modified — renders two labelled sections: Movies then TV Shows)

what_the_diff_does.before: My Orders screen renders all orders in a single flat list with no grouping. Orders for movies and TV shows appear mixed together.
what_the_diff_does.after: My Orders screen renders two labelled sections: 'Movies' (type='Movie' orders) followed by 'TV Shows' (type='TVShow' orders). Each section header is bold and displayed above its rows. Sections with zero items are hidden.
what_the_diff_does.visible_change: The My Orders screen now shows a 'Movies' heading above movie purchases and a 'TV Shows' heading above TV show purchases, making it easier to find a specific order at a glance.
what_the_diff_does.compile_note: The import line in MyOrder.js is extended to also import ORDER_TYPES and getOrdersByType from the same file, so no new dependency files are introduced.

CRITICAL.reason: orders-clustering.feature.test.js declares both changed files as @source and contains describe blocks that match every changed symbol: ORDER_TYPES (4 tests), getOrdersByType (7 tests), myOrderDetails type field (6 tests), Section grouping (5 tests). All 22 are CRITICAL.
tests: ORDER_TYPES > MOVIE value is the string "Movie"
ORDER_TYPES > TV_SHOW value is the string "TVShow"
ORDER_TYPES > contains exactly two keys
ORDER_TYPES > MOVIE and TV_SHOW are distinct values
getOrdersByType > returns only Movie orders when type is ORDER_TYPES.MOVIE
getOrdersByType > returns only TVShow orders when type is ORDER_TYPES.TV_SHOW
getOrdersByType > returns empty array when no orders match the type
getOrdersByType > returns empty array when orders list is empty
getOrdersByType > returns empty array when orders argument is undefined
getOrdersByType > returns empty array when orders argument is null
getOrdersByType > does not mutate the original orders array
myOrderDetails — type field > every entry in myOrderDetails has a type field
myOrderDetails — type field > type field is either Movie or TVShow for every entry
myOrderDetails — type field > Spider Man is classified as Movie
myOrderDetails — type field > Avatar is classified as Movie
myOrderDetails — type field > Wonder Woman is classified as TVShow
myOrderDetails — type field > Thor is classified as TVShow
Section grouping — Movies before TV Shows > render order array has Movies as the first entry
Section grouping — Movies before TV Shows > render order array has TV Shows as the second entry
Section grouping — Movies before TV Shows > Movie group has 3 items in sample data
Section grouping — Movies before TV Shows > TV Show group has 2 items in sample data
Section grouping — Movies before TV Shows > Movie + TV Show counts sum to total order count

HIGH.reason: wishlist.feature.test.js and favourites-watchlist.feature.test.js cover the broader order/watchlist data layer — these must be re-verified to confirm the type field addition did not break any existing data contracts.

SKIP.reason: All remaining test files do not declare myOrderData.js or MyOrder.js as a source and do not test order clustering logic.

coverage_gaps.gap: No existing tests covered this new constant.
No existing tests covered this new helper function.
No existing tests validated the type field on each order entry.

jest_run_note: orders-clustering.feature.test.js runs 22 CRITICAL tests for ORDER_TYPES, getOrdersByType, and section grouping. wishlist and favourites-watchlist files run 28 HIGH tests verifying no regressions in the data layer.
